original text:
The GasSuf exhibition will run from 22 till 24 October 2019 in Pavilions 4 at the Sokolniki ECC.
The cost of the ticket in booking office is 500 rubles.
Metro station "Sokolniki" (Sokolnicheskaya line, red 1), the only exits to from the station, further walk to the Sokolniki park.
The exhibition centre is located within the Sokolniki park territory which is in 10 minutes' walk from the metro station.
The entry of cars is accessible through the 3rd Luchevoy Prosek.
The cost of the entry by motocycle is 200 rubles; car is 500 rubles.
You can leave your car at a specially equipped parking.
The cost of the entry by limousine, minibus, car with trailer are 700 rubles; buses, trucks, etc.
are 1400 rubles; by multitran, tow truck, loader, crane are 6500 rubles.

original text:
Work with Bill Richard in Savannah, GA to get a business insurance quote and choose what products are right for you.
Bill Richard in Savannah, GA offers a State Farm® Personal Articles Policy.
Add this to your property insurance to help cover your personal property against theft, accidental damage, or loss.
Ready to get started?
Bill Richard in Savannah, GA can help you learn about State Farm Bank® and mobile banking.
State Farm® offers various types of affordable family health insurance.
Bill Richard in Savannah, GA can help you choose the health insurance plan that's best for you.
Talk to Bill Richard in Savannah, GA to get annuity information and find the fund that meets your needs.
Contact Agent Bill Richard in Savannah, GA for investment information.
There's a lot to learn when you're getting started with life insurance.
When you're ready, Bill Richard in Savannah, GA is here to help.
Shopping for car insurance?
Bill Richard in Savannah, GA is one of more than 18,000 State Farm® agents nationwide who can help keep you and your family covered with quality auto insurance.
You can talk to an agent at any time.
Make the move with Bill Richard in Savannah, GA to protect your home and family.
Plus, save money when you have both car and home insurance with us.
There's a lot to learn when you're getting started with life insurance.
When you're ready, Bill Richard in Savannah, GA is here to help.

original text:
Edward II (25 April 1284 - 21 September 1327), also called Edward of Caernarfon, was King of England from 1307 until he was deposed in January 1327.
The fourth son of Edward I, Edward became the heir to the throne following the death of his older brother Alphonso.
Beginning in 1300, Edward accompanied his father on campaigns to pacify Scotland, and in 1306 he was knighted in a grand ceremony at Westminster Abbey.
Edward succeeded to the throne in 1307, following his father's death.
In 1308, he married Isabella of France, the daughter of the powerful King Philip IV, as part of a long-running effort to resolve the tensions between the English and French crowns.
Edward had a close and controversial relationship with Piers Gaveston, who had joined his household in 1300.
The precise nature of Edward and Gaveston's relationship is uncertain; they may have been friends, lovers or sworn brothers.
Gaveston's arrogance and power as Edward's favourite provoked discontent both among the barons and the French royal family, and Edward was forced to exile him.
On Gaveston's return, the barons pressured the King into agreeing to wide-ranging reforms called the Ordinances of 1311.
The newly empowered barons banished Gaveston, to which Edward responded by revoking the reforms and recalling his favourite.
Led by Edward's cousin, the Earl of Lancaster, a group of the barons seized and executed Gaveston in 1312, beginning several years of armed confrontation.
English forces were pushed back in Scotland, where Edward was decisively defeated by Robert the Bruce at the Battle of Bannockburn in 1314.
Widespread famine followed, and criticism of the King's reign mounted.
Edward II (known also as EII, and previously as Edward the Second and the Red Hot Polkas and e2K) are an English band which play a fusion of world music, English folk and reggae.
Active from 1980, the band broke up after losing several key members in 1999, relaunching as "e2K" in 2000.
In 2003, the band dissolved once more, but have since reformed for a one year reunion tour in 2009 under the "Edward II" name, celebrating the tenth anniversary of the breakup of the original band.
They reformed in 2015 specifically to produce the Manchester's Improving Daily project, which includes the release of new recorded material and a book.
The project is designed to celebrate a collection of tunes written in Manchester during the Industrial Revolution, published as Broadsides and currently held in Manchester Central Library.
The band formed in Cheltenham, Gloucestershire in 1980, in order to play for monthly dances at The Victory Club.
Founder members Lizzy Howe-Pellant (melodeon) and Dion Cochrane (tenor banjo) were joined by Paul Burgess, Floss Headford and John Gill (fiddles), Martin Brinsford (sax) and Dave Haines (melodeon and concertina), and bringing in Richard Valentine on piano and as caller.
It ran for a year or so in that form.
Edward II of England was the King of England from 1307 till 1327.
Edward the Martyr, King of England from 975 to 979, who might arguably be regarded as "Edward II".
Edward II is a Renaissance or Early Modern period play written by Christopher Marlowe.
It is one of the earliest English history plays.
The full title of the first publication is The Troublesome Reign and Lamentable Death of Edward the Second, King of England, with the Tragical Fall of Proud Mortimer.
Marlowe found most of his material for this play in the third volume of Raphael Holinshed's Chronicles (1587).
Frederick Boas believes that "out of all the rich material provided by Holinshed" Marlowe was drawn to "the comparatively unattractive reign of Edward II" due to the relationship between the King and Gaveston.
Boas elaborates, "Homosexual affection ... has (as has been seen) a special attraction for Marlowe.
Jove and Ganymede in Dido, Henry III and his 'minions' in The Massacre, Neptune and Leander in Hero and Leander, and all akin, although drawn to a slighter scale, to Edward and Gaveston."
Boas also notes the existence of a number of parallels between Edward II and The Massacre at Paris, asserting that "it is scarcely too much to say that scenes xi-xxi of The Massacre are something in the nature of a preliminary sketch for Edward II."
Marlowe stayed close to the account but embellished it with the character of Lightborn (or Lucifer) as Edward's assassin.
England were a progressive rock group active in the late 1970s, and briefly reformed in 2006.
The band is notable for their album Garden Shed released on Arista Records, and for keyboardist Robert Webb playing a Mellotron sawn in half.
The band was formed in 1975 by drummer Mark Ibbotson, and, after a variety of prototype groups, stabilised around a line-up of himself, bassist Martin Henderson, guitarist Jamie Moses and keyboardist Robert Webb.
Moses and Webb had previously collaborated on an unreleased album.
Ibbottson owned a Mk II Mellotron, which Webb took an interest in, later saying "it opens up possibilities.
It's having control like the conductor of an orchestra."
To make the instrument portable, Webb sawed the instrument in half, rehousing the left-hand manual and the right-hand tapes (which contained the lead sounds on a Mk II model) it in a new case.
After a number of gigs at the Hazlitt Theatre in Maidstone, Moses quit and was replaced by Franc Holland.
In March 1976, immediately following a showcase gig that resulted in a contract with Arista Records, Ibbotson quit the band and was replaced by Jode Leigh.

original text:
Cronforce is an online project management software with timesheets and budget tracking tool.
It can be utilized by freelancers and small companies as well as larger firms with smaller departments.
Cronforce is a cloud-based software that can be used by project managers, HR managers, and finance managers.
It offers modules, dashboards, and powerful analytics features to convert data into understandable graphics.
Cronforce automates business processes to help manage resources, budgets, and time.
There are well-liked and widely used solutions in each software category.
But are they automatically the best fit for your organization's special wants?
A market-leading software product may have thousands of customers, but does it provide what you need?
For this reason, do not blindly shell out for popular systems.
Read at least a few Cronforce reviews and consider the aspects that you wish to have in the software such as the cost, main features, available integrations etc.
Then, select a few apps that fit your requirements.
Try out the free trials of these platforms, read online comments, get explanations from the vendor, and do your investigation thoroughly.
This in-depth groundwork is certain to help you select the finest software platform for your organization's unique requirements.
How Much Does Cronforce Cost?
We realize that when you make a decision to buy Project Management Software it's important not only to see how experts evaluate it in their reviews, but also to find out if the real people and companies that buy it are actually satisfied with the product.
That's why we've created our behavior-based Customer Satisfaction AlgorithmTM that gathers customer reviews, comments and Cronforce reviews across a wide range of social media sites.
The data is then presented in an easy to digest form showing how many people had positive and negative experience with Cronforce.
With that information at hand you should be equipped to make an informed buying decision that you won't regret.
What are Cronforce pricing details?
What integrations are available for Cronforce?
If you need more information about Cronforce or you simply have a question let us know what you need and we'll forward your request directly to the vendor.

original text:
On Thursday it will be mainly cloudless during the day.
In the evening mostly cloudy weather will prevail.
Then it will become overcast, but mainly dry.
Morning temperatures will be around 4 °C.
During the day they will reach a maximum of 12 °C.
Low temperatures at night will be around 0°C.
Southeasterly winds are brisk with stormy gusts, in the late evening moderate.
On Friday it will be overcast with snowfall until noon.
The noon will bring variable clouds with snow showers.
During the night mostly cloudy weather will prevail.
Morning temperatures will be around freezing point and will reach a maximum of 3 °C during the day.
Low temperatures at night will be around 1°C.
Southerly winds are weak.

Morning temperatures will be around 4 °C.
During the day they will reach a maximum of 12 °C.
Low temperatures at night will be around 0°C.
Southeasterly winds are brisk with stormy gusts, in the late evening moderate.
On Friday it will be overcast with snowfall until noon.
The noon will bring variable clouds with snow showers.
During the night mostly cloudy weather will prevail.
Morning temperatures will be around freezing point and will reach a maximum of 3 °C during the day.
Low temperatures at night will be around 1°C.
Southerly winds are weak.

original text:
Partnerships have gained favor in Thoroughbred Racing circles.
Easy: You can share the cost and enjoy the benefits of the sport of kings without the high costs of individual ownership.
Ok, but what is the downside?
Partnerships charge high management fees and the monthly costs can be exorbitant.
This cancels out the benefits mentioned above.
Many of the leading partnerships win big races but partners reap no monetary benefits.
We feel this is wrong and strive to create partnerships that can allow partners to make money.
I offer a partnership unlike any other.
I can manage every aspect for you and your group.
I will find the horses, select trainers and deal with all the details.
You are free to have fun.
I receive a share of the horse, and a % of winnings, but charge only miniscule management fees.
I work with a general partner from your group, going over bills, and explaining things along the way.
As web development is one of our services, we also build your partnership a website to allow partners to stay up to date at all times!
So you are free to have fun, the way racing should be!

This cancels out the benefits mentioned above.
Many of the leading partnerships win big races but partners reap no monetary benefits.
We feel this is wrong and strive to create partnerships that can allow partners to make money.
I offer a partnership unlike any other.
I can manage every aspect for you and your group.
I will find the horses, select trainers and deal with all the details.
You are free to have fun.
As web development is one of our services, we also build your partnership a website to allow partners to stay up to date at all times!
So you are free to have fun, the way racing should be!

original text:
Snooping in medicine cabinets used to be a lot more informing.
(Uh, not that we've snooped.)
Anyways, our point: A rising number of medicines are being used for reasons other than what it says on the label.
Unlike prescription drug abuse of the past, this isn't all about the latest party drugs.
Today, people are lured toward medicines for unique psychological benefits - whether to perk up a dud personality or calm jittery nerves.
While pill-poppers have gotten loopy on codeine-based medications for years, now it's common for people to pop heart medicine to cure test anxiety or load up on narcolepsy meds to crank out a presentation.
Even the Food and Drug Administration can't keep up with drug companies hawking the marketable side effects of their remedies.
A new report from the Kaiser Institute shows it takes the FDA 7 months to issue a warning for this type of illegal marketing, and companies average 4 months to take action.
As a result, more people are popping pills for ailments for which drugs weren't tested and approved to treat - and patient safety is diminished.
Before you get caught in a web of mixed medicines, consult this review of what's showing up in medicine cabinets, what they can do to you, and alternatives that are side-effect free.
What does it do?
Creates a euphoric sense similar to cocaine.
Improves mood, fatigue, sleepiness and cognition like caffeine, but for a longer period of time.
Helps promote wakefulness and alertness.
Who gets prescribed this?
Patients with Attention Deficit Disorder (ADD), Attention Deficit Hyperactive Disorder (ADHD), and narcolepsy.
Provigil can also help with obstructive sleep apnea and is prescribed to elderly patients who need help waking up in the morning.
Who abuses this?
Stimulants are the performance-enhancing drugs of the corporate world.
Wall Street professionals and lawyers are said to like Provigil because of its easy accessibility, adding to it's appeal - this drug is less regulated than Ritalin and Adderall.
Late-night partiers may also rely on these to get through the next day, says Dr. Tucker Woods, Chairman of the Department of Emergency Medicine at Long Island College Hospital of Brooklyn.
Addictive?
Stimulants can be habit forming if patients rely on them for energy.
Alternative medicine: Because doctors recommend trying to minimize medications at all cost, stick with coffee.
It tastes better, and your co-workers can't complain about you juicing.
Other alternatives include natural stimulants like Ginseng, available at most health stores, although its effectiveness depends on the amount of extract, the type of ginseng, and the company that makes it.
The Panax (also called Asian or Chinese) variety seems to work the best.
What does it do?
Used to calm nerves, prevent anxiety attacks, and as a sleep aid.
Who gets prescribed this?
Insomniacs and patients with anxiety problems like obsessive compulsive disorder (OCD) or social phobias.
It can also be used to relax the muscles in patients with cerebral palsy or multiple sclerosis.
Who abuses this?
These drugs create a calming effect, and recreate the effects of feeling drunk.
Those who abuse this will lapse further into a more relaxed state.
Desired by thrill-seekers wanting to intensify a drunken night out, these types of drugs have an additive effect when combined with alcohol, amplifying the effects and possibly causing death.
"Alcohol slows down your breathing and benzodiazepines slow down your breathing in a different way - the combine effect can kill you," says Philip Wenger, PharmD, assistant professor at St. Louis College of Pharmacy.
Addictive?
Yes, when used chronically.
Alternative medicine: If stress is what's eating you, try to make a habit of mid-afternoon workouts.
A study by researchers at the University of Missouri-Columbia found high-intensity exercise greatly reduces stress and anxiety.
Making sleep a routine can also help: try to get to bed at the same time and wake up at the same time every night.
The herbal supplement, melatonin, is sometimes used for sleep and research shows it can help you nod off - especially if you're suffering from jet-lag.
What does it do?
Antidepressants stimulate chemical changes that increase the levels of neurotransmitters associated with mood, including serotonin, norepinephrine and dopamine.
Who abuses this?
Some use antidepressants as a quick-fix for moodiness, which critics say would be better addressed with a healthy diet and getting a full 8 hours of sleep.
Alternative medicine: Talk to a therapist and see if your depression is something you can work out through discussion, rather than meds.
For over the counter stuff, St. John's Wort is a supplement sometimes taken for depression, but be careful because it can interact with post-surgery medicine and some high blood pressure prescriptions  - so check with your doctor first.
Or you can eat your way to a good mood with grilled fish, like salmon.
A study in Finland found that people who eat more fish are 31 percent less likely to suffer from depression because they contain feel-good omega-3 fatty acids.
What does it do?
These sedatives relax the body by binding to neurotransmitters in the brain, producing a calming effect.
stimulants such as amphetamines (including methamphetamine), cocaine, and ecstasy.
Some people take it also to get a high - they will fight the sleeping affects and try to stay awake because of the sensation it gives them, which include vivid visuals and a body high.
Addictive?
This drug only takes about 15 minutes to kick in, so it can be habit-forming.
Negative side affects can include hallucinations and forgetfulness.
Alternative medicine: Try a natural sleep aid, like melatonin, or an herbal supplement like Valerian.
Or try some yoga poses before bed to stretch the body and relax the mind.
What does it do?
This type of heart medication is used to treat high blood pressure and migraines.
It's been used to treat anxiety since the 70s.
Beta blockers steady a shaking hand and slow a pounding heart by blocking adrenaline-like chemicals in the system, forcing the heart to beat slowly.
Who gets prescribed this?
Used to treat patients with high blood pressure.
Performers and musicians can also get a prescription to cure stage fright.
Who abuses this?
Since the off-label use isn't harmful, musicians, public speakers and surgeons use this to calm nerves and steady the hand.
Addictive?
Beta blockers are non-habit forming.

Some people take it also to get a high - they will fight the sleeping affects and try to stay awake because of the sensation it gives them, which include vivid visuals and a body high.
Addictive?
This drug only takes about 15 minutes to kick in, so it can be habit-forming.
Negative side affects can include hallucinations and forgetfulness.
What does it do?
This type of heart medication is used to treat high blood pressure and migraines.
It's been used to treat anxiety since the 70s.
Beta blockers steady a shaking hand and slow a pounding heart by blocking adrenaline-like chemicals in the system, forcing the heart to beat slowly.
Who gets prescribed this?
Used to treat patients with high blood pressure.
Performers and musicians can also get a prescription to cure stage fright.
Who abuses this?
Since the off-label use isn't harmful, musicians, public speakers and surgeons use this to calm nerves and steady the hand.
Addictive?
Beta blockers are non-habit forming.

Place a sheet of paper on your working surface this will be the base of your basket.
Then take the egg tray and place it on the paper.
Start making the sides of the basket, out of the newspaper.
Roll the newspaper as shown in the picture and make the sides.
Then add the rolled newspaper to the basket to create grids and start braiding them one into each other to make the pattern of the basket.
If you get lost in the process, just follow the pictures.
When your basket is done, you can paint it any color you like.
Fill it with eggs and add some natural grass as decoration.
is this available any where with full instructions?
I don't see what they did with the strips under the paper, did they just glue the paper on without weaving any of the strips?
and i'd like to see better details on how they braided the top rail.

original text:
HO Scale Back ground building facade.
Laser cut walls, easy to follow templates and well written and illustrated instructions.
Every town that was served by the railroad had a local hotel or boarding house.
Over the years when the railroads didn't stop anymore these hotels became rooming houses.
The Sycamore Springs Hotel is a back ground, designed to complete your downtown scenes!
Footprint is 8 3/4" x 1 3/4".
